sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.70 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Let$$be an automorphism of prime order$q$of an infinite free Burnside group$G=B(q,p)$of prime exponent$p$such that$$cyclically permutes the free generators of$G$. Is it true that$$fixes some non-trivial element of$G$? Editors' comment: this is proved for$q=2$in (V.\,S.\,Atabekyan, H.\,T.\,Aslanyan, Proc. Yerevan State Univ. Physic. Math. Sci., 53, no.~3 (2019), 147--149). { V.\,D.\,Mazurov}

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
